paths.karma_yoga: Path of Selfless Action — for career, duty, purpose, work-life balance
paths.bhakti_yoga: Path of Devotion — for relationships, love, trust, emotional healing
paths.jnana_yoga: Path of Knowledge — for self-awareness, clarity, confusion, decision-making
paths.dhyana_yoga: Path of Meditation — for inner peace, anxiety, stress, mental health
paths.sankhya_yoga: Path of Wisdom — for grief, loss, detachment, understanding life/death
paths.raja_yoga: Path of Discipline — for health, habits, self-control, willpower

slokas.sanskrit: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन। मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥
slokas.transliteration: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana | mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo 'stv akarmaṇi ||
slokas.english_meaning: You have the right to perform your duty, but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions. Never consider yourself the cause of the results, and never be attached to inaction.
slokas.when_to_use: When you are anxious about career outcomes, promotion results, or feel frustrated that hard work isn't paying off.

slokas.sanskrit: मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः। आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत॥
slokas.transliteration: mātrā-sparśās tu kaunteya śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ | āgamāpāyino 'nityās tāṁs titikṣasva bhārata ||
slokas.english_meaning: The contact of the senses with their objects gives rise to cold, heat, pleasure, and pain. They are transient — they come and go. Bear them patiently, O Bharata.
slokas.when_to_use: When going through a difficult phase — job loss, breakup, health issues — and need to remember that this too shall pass.

slokas.sanskrit: ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते। सङ्गात्सञ्जायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते॥
slokas.transliteration: dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣūpajāyate | saṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho 'bhijāyate ||
slokas.english_meaning: Dwelling on sense objects breeds attachment; attachment breeds desire; desire breeds anger.
slokas.when_to_use: When struggling with anger, jealousy, obsessive thoughts, or unhealthy attachments to people or outcomes.

slokas.sanskrit: क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः। स्मृतिभ्रंशाद्बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति॥
slokas.transliteration: krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ sammohāt smṛti-vibhramaḥ | smṛti-bhraṁśād buddhi-nāśo buddhi-nāśāt praṇaśyati ||
slokas.english_meaning: From anger comes delusion; from delusion, loss of memory; from loss of memory, destruction of intellect; from destruction of intellect, one perishes.
slokas.when_to_use: When anger is leading you to make bad decisions or when you feel you are losing control over your emotions.

slokas.sanskrit: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्। स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः॥
slokas.transliteration: śreyān sva-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣṭhitāt | sva-dharme nidhanaṁ śreyaḥ para-dharmo bhayāvahaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: It is better to perform one's own duty imperfectly than to perform another's duty perfectly. It is better to die fulfilling one's own duty; the duty of another is fraught with danger.
slokas.when_to_use: When comparing yourself to others, feeling lost about your career path, or trying to be someone you're not.

slokas.sanskrit: उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्। आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः॥
slokas.transliteration: uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmānam avasādayet | ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Elevate yourself by your own mind, and do not degrade yourself. The mind alone is the friend of the self, and the mind alone is the enemy of the self.
slokas.when_to_use: When battling self-doubt, depression, or feeling like your own worst enemy. A reminder that you have the power to lift yourself.

slokas.sanskrit: असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम्। अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते॥
slokas.transliteration: asaṁśayaṁ mahā-bāho mano durnigrahaṁ calam | abhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa ca gṛhyate ||
slokas.english_meaning: Undoubtedly, O mighty-armed, the mind is restless and very difficult to control. But it can be restrained through practice and detachment.
slokas.when_to_use: When your mind won't stop racing, you can't focus, or anxiety is overwhelming. Practice and patience are the answer.

slokas.sanskrit: आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत्। तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी॥
slokas.transliteration: āpūryamāṇam acala-pratiṣṭhaṁ samudram āpaḥ praviśanti yadvat | tadvat kāmā yaṁ praviśanti sarve sa śāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī ||
slokas.english_meaning: Just as the ocean remains undisturbed by the incessant flow of rivers, so the person who is unmoved by desires attains peace — not the one who strives to satisfy desires.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling restless from wanting more — more money, more success, more validation. True peace comes from stillness within.

slokas.sanskrit: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत। अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्॥
slokas.transliteration: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata | abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṁ sṛjāmy aham ||
slokas.english_meaning: Whenever there is a decline in righteousness and an increase in unrighteousness, at that time I manifest Myself.
slokas.when_to_use: When the world feels unjust, when bad things happen to good people, and you need faith that things will be set right.

slokas.sanskrit: अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते। तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्॥
slokas.transliteration: ananyāś cintayanto māṁ ye janāḥ paryupāsate | teṣāṁ nityābhiyuktānāṁ yoga-kṣemaṁ vahāmy aham ||
slokas.english_meaning: To those who worship Me with undivided devotion, meditating on Me always, I carry what they lack and preserve what they have.
slokas.when_to_use: When worrying about financial security, survival, or feeling alone in your struggles. A promise of divine support.

slokas.sanskrit: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज। अहं त्वां सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥
slokas.transliteration: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja | ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Abandon all varieties of duty and simply surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.
slokas.when_to_use: When overwhelmed with guilt, fear, or the weight of trying to control everything. The ultimate message of letting go.

slokas.sanskrit: वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि। तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णान्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही॥
slokas.transliteration: vāsāṁsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya navāni gṛhṇāti naro 'parāṇi | tathā śarīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇāny anyāni saṁyāti navāni dehī ||
slokas.english_meaning: As a person puts on new garments, giving up old ones, the soul similarly accepts new material bodies, giving up the old and useless ones.
slokas.when_to_use: When dealing with the death of a loved one or fear of death. A perspective on the eternal nature of the soul.

slokas.sanskrit: क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते। क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप॥
slokas.transliteration: klaibyaṁ mā sma gamaḥ pārtha naitat tvayy upapadyate | kṣudraṁ hṛdaya-daurbalyaṁ tyaktvottiṣṭha parantapa ||
slokas.english_meaning: Do not yield to weakness, O Partha. It does not befit you. Shake off this petty faintheartedness and arise, O scorcher of enemies!
slokas.when_to_use: When you feel like giving up, when fear paralyzes you, or when you need a push to take bold action.

slokas.sanskrit: न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते। तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति॥
slokas.transliteration: na hi jñānena sadṛśaṁ pavitram iha vidyate | tat svayaṁ yoga-saṁsiddhaḥ kālenātmani vindati ||
slokas.english_meaning: There is nothing in this world as purifying as knowledge. One who has attained perfection in yoga finds this knowledge within the self, in due course of time.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling lost or confused about your path. Keep learning — knowledge purifies and clarity comes with time.

slokas.sanskrit: युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु। युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दुःखहा॥
slokas.transliteration: yuktāhāra-vihārasya yukta-ceṣṭasya karmasu | yukta-svapnāvabodhasya yogo bhavati duḥkha-hā ||
slokas.english_meaning: For one who is moderate in eating, recreation, working, sleeping, and waking, yoga destroys all suffering.
slokas.when_to_use: When health is suffering due to overwork, poor diet, or lack of sleep. Balance in all things is the cure.

slokas.sanskrit: यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः। स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते॥
slokas.transliteration: yad yad ācarati śreṣṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ | sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate ||
slokas.english_meaning: Whatever a great person does, common people follow. Whatever standards they set, the whole world pursues.
slokas.when_to_use: When in a leadership position or when your actions affect others. Lead by example.

slokas.sanskrit: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च। निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी॥
slokas.transliteration: adveṣṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva ca | nirmamo nirahaṅkāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣamī ||
slokas.english_meaning: One who is free from malice toward all beings, friendly and compassionate, free from possessiveness and ego, equal in joy and sorrow, and forgiving.
slokas.when_to_use: When struggling in relationships, holding grudges, or when ego is causing conflicts with loved ones.

slokas.sanskrit: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय। सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते॥
slokas.transliteration: yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjaya | siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga ucyate ||
slokas.english_meaning: Perform your duty steadfast in yoga, abandoning attachment. Be equal in success and failure, for such equanimity is called yoga.
slokas.when_to_use: When obsessing over success or crushed by failure. True balance means performing your best without being enslaved by results.

slokas.sanskrit: ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः। लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा॥
slokas.transliteration: brahmaṇy ādhāya karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā karoti yaḥ | lipyate na sa pāpena padma-patram ivāmbhasā ||
slokas.english_meaning: One who acts without attachment, dedicating all actions to the divine, is not tainted by sin — just as a lotus leaf is untouched by water.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling tainted by the environments you work in or the compromises life demands. Stay pure like the lotus.

slokas.sanskrit: यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत्। यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम्॥
slokas.transliteration: yat karoṣi yad aśnāsi yaj juhoṣi dadāsi yat | yat tapasyasi kaunteya tat kuruṣva mad-arpaṇam ||
slokas.english_meaning: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer or give away, whatever austerity you perform — do that as an offering to Me.
slokas.when_to_use: When daily life feels meaningless or routine. Every action can become sacred when done with intention.

slokas.sanskrit: तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व जित्वा शत्रून्भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम्। मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन्॥
slokas.transliteration: tasmāt tvam uttiṣṭha yaśo labhasva jitvā śatrūn bhuṅkṣva rājyaṁ samṛddham | mayaivaite nihatāḥ pūrvam eva nimitta-mātraṁ bhava savyasācin ||
slokas.english_meaning: Therefore, arise and attain glory. Conquer your enemies and enjoy a prosperous kingdom. They are already slain by My arrangement; you are merely an instrument.
slokas.when_to_use: When hesitating to seize an opportunity or afraid to take the next step. The path is already laid — just act.

slokas.sanskrit: दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः। वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते॥
slokas.transliteration: duḥkheṣv anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣu vigata-spṛhaḥ | vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthita-dhīr munir ucyate ||
slokas.english_meaning: One whose mind is undisturbed in sorrow, who does not crave pleasure, and who is free from attachment, fear, and anger — is called a sage of steady wisdom.
slokas.when_to_use: When emotions are all over the place — too happy, too sad, too angry. The goal is steady wisdom, not suppression.

slokas.sanskrit: कर्मण्यकर्म यः पश्येदकर्मणि च कर्म यः। स बुद्धिमान्मनुष्येषु स युक्तः कृत्स्नकर्मकृत्॥
slokas.transliteration: karmaṇy akarma yaḥ paśyed akarmaṇi ca karma yaḥ | sa buddhimān manuṣyeṣu sa yuktaḥ kṛtsna-karma-kṛt ||
slokas.english_meaning: One who sees inaction in action and action in inaction is truly wise among humans and is spiritually connected, even while performing all kinds of activities.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling that you're always busy but achieving nothing, or that rest is laziness. True wisdom is seeing the deeper reality.

slokas.sanskrit: प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः। अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते॥
slokas.transliteration: prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ | ahaṅkāra-vimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate ||
slokas.english_meaning: All activities are performed by the three modes of material nature. But one deluded by ego thinks: 'I am the doer.'
slokas.when_to_use: When you think everything depends on you alone, or when ego is inflated. Let go of the illusion of being the sole doer.

slokas.sanskrit: प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव। न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति॥
slokas.transliteration: prakāśaṁ ca pravṛttiṁ ca moham eva ca pāṇḍava | na dveṣṭi sampravṛttāni na nivṛttāni kāṅkṣati ||
slokas.english_meaning: O Pandava, one who does not hate illumination, activity, or delusion when they appear, nor longs for them when they disappear.
slokas.when_to_use: When resisting natural phases of life — sometimes you're motivated, sometimes lazy, sometimes confused. Accept all phases.

slokas.sanskrit: यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः। हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः॥
slokas.transliteration: yasmān nodvijate loko lokān nodvijate ca yaḥ | harṣāmarṣa-bhayodvegair mukto yaḥ sa ca me priyaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: One by whom the world is not disturbed, and who is not disturbed by the world, who is free from joy, envy, fear, and anxiety — is dear to Me.
slokas.when_to_use: When other people's opinions are controlling your peace, or when you feel socially anxious and overwhelmed.

slokas.sanskrit: यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम्। तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम्॥
slokas.transliteration: yat tad agre viṣam iva pariṇāme 'mṛtopamam | tat sukhaṁ sāttvikaṁ proktam ātma-buddhi-prasāda-jam ||
slokas.english_meaning: That which feels like poison in the beginning but is like nectar in the end — that happiness born of self-realization is said to be sattvic (pure).
slokas.when_to_use: When struggling to stick to a good habit — exercise, study, diet. The hardest things yield the sweetest results.

slokas.sanskrit: विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम्। परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम्॥
slokas.transliteration: viṣayendriya-saṁyogād yat tad agre 'mṛtopamam | pariṇāme viṣam iva tat sukhaṁ rājasaṁ smṛtam ||
slokas.english_meaning: That happiness which arises from the contact of the senses with their objects — which feels like nectar at first but is like poison in the end — is said to be rajasic.
slokas.when_to_use: When tempted by short-term pleasures that you know will hurt later — junk food, procrastination, toxic relationships.

slokas.sanskrit: यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता। योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः॥
slokas.transliteration: yathā dīpo nivāta-stho neṅgate sopamā smṛtā | yogino yata-cittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: As a lamp in a windless place does not flicker — that is the simile for a yogi whose mind is disciplined in meditation.
slokas.when_to_use: When seeking focus and stillness. A beautiful image to hold during meditation practice.

slokas.sanskrit: विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः। निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः स शान्तिमधिगच्छति॥
slokas.transliteration: vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumāṁś carati niḥspṛhaḥ | nirmamo nirahaṅkāraḥ sa śāntim adhigacchati ||
slokas.english_meaning: A person who has given up all desires, who lives free from longing, who is without possessiveness and ego — attains perfect peace.
slokas.when_to_use: When material possessions and ego are weighing you down. True peace is in simplicity.

slokas.sanskrit: चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन। आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ॥
slokas.transliteration: catur-vidhā bhajante māṁ janāḥ sukṛtino 'rjuna | ārto jijñāsur arthārthī jñānī ca bharatarṣabha ||
slokas.english_meaning: Four kinds of pious people worship Me: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of wealth, and the wise.
slokas.when_to_use: When questioning why you turn to spirituality — whether in pain, curiosity, need, or wisdom. All paths are valid.

slokas.sanskrit: त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः। कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत्॥
slokas.transliteration: tri-vidhaṁ narakasyedaṁ dvāraṁ nāśanam ātmanaḥ | kāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayaṁ tyajet ||
slokas.english_meaning: There are three gates to self-destruction and hell: lust, anger, and greed. One must abandon all three.
slokas.when_to_use: When caught in a cycle of desire, rage, or greed that's ruining your life. Identify which gate you're walking through.

slokas.sanskrit: अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत्। स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते॥
slokas.transliteration: anudvega-karaṁ vākyaṁ satyaṁ priya-hitaṁ ca yat | svādhyāyābhyasanaṁ caiva vāṅ-mayaṁ tapa ucyate ||
slokas.english_meaning: Speech that does not cause distress, that is truthful, pleasant, and beneficial, and the practice of self-study — this is called austerity of speech.
slokas.when_to_use: When your words are hurting others or when you struggle with honest yet kind communication.

slokas.sanskrit: ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते। आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः॥
slokas.transliteration: ye hi saṁsparśajā bhogā duḥkha-yonaya eva te | ādy-antavantaḥ kaunteya na teṣu ramate budhaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Pleasures born of sense contact are sources of suffering. They have a beginning and an end. The wise do not delight in them.
slokas.when_to_use: When chasing pleasures that leave you emptier than before — shopping, social media, validation. Look deeper.

slokas.sanskrit: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर। असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः॥
slokas.transliteration: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samācara | asakto hy ācaran karma param āpnoti pūruṣaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Therefore, always perform your duty without attachment. By working without attachment, one attains the Supreme.
slokas.when_to_use: When work feels meaningless or you're burnt out. Do the work, but release attachment to outcomes.

slokas.sanskrit: समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम्। विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति॥
slokas.transliteration: samaṁ sarveṣu bhūteṣu tiṣṭhantaṁ parameśvaram | vinaśyatsv avinaśyantaṁ yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati ||
slokas.english_meaning: One who sees the Supreme Lord dwelling equally in all beings — the imperishable within the perishable — truly sees.
slokas.when_to_use: When judging others, feeling superior/inferior, or struggling with discrimination. All beings carry the same divine spark.

slokas.sanskrit: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु। मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः॥
slokas.transliteration: man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru | mām evaiṣyasi yuktvaivam ātmānaṁ mat-parāyaṇaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, worship Me, and bow to Me. Thus uniting yourself with Me and setting Me as your supreme goal, you shall come to Me.
slokas.when_to_use: When seeking a simple, direct spiritual practice. The essence of bhakti — think of the divine, always.

slokas.sanskrit: सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत्। सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः॥
slokas.transliteration: sahajaṁ karma kaunteya sa-doṣam api na tyajet | sarvārambhā hi doṣeṇa dhūmenāgnir ivāvṛtāḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: One should not abandon one's natural duty, even if it has flaws — for all undertakings are covered by faults, as fire is covered by smoke.
slokas.when_to_use: When perfectionism stops you from acting. Nothing is flawless — do it anyway.

slokas.sanskrit: समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः। शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः॥
slokas.transliteration: samaḥ śatrau ca mitre ca tathā mānāpamānayoḥ | śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣu samaḥ saṅga-vivarjitaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Equal toward friend and foe, in honor and dishonor, in heat and cold, in pleasure and pain, free from attachment.
slokas.when_to_use: When someone's praise inflates you or someone's insult crushes you. Aim for equanimity in all situations.

slokas.sanskrit: निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः। द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञैर्गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत्॥
slokas.transliteration: nirmāna-mohā jita-saṅga-doṣā adhyātma-nityā vinivṛtta-kāmāḥ | dvandvair vimuktāḥ sukha-duḥkha-saṁjñair gacchanty amūḍhāḥ padam avyayaṁ tat ||
slokas.english_meaning: Free from pride and delusion, victorious over attachment, dwelling constantly in the Self, desires fully stilled, liberated from dualities of pleasure and pain — the undeluded reach that eternal state.
slokas.when_to_use: When seeking the ultimate goal of spiritual life — a vision of what true freedom looks like.

slokas.sanskrit: इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया। विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु॥
slokas.transliteration: iti te jñānam ākhyātaṁ guhyād guhyataraṁ mayā | vimṛśyaitad aśeṣeṇa yathecchasi tathā kuru ||
slokas.english_meaning: Thus I have explained to you the most confidential of all knowledge. Deliberate on it fully, and then do as you wish.
slokas.when_to_use: When someone is pushing you toward a decision. Even God gives you free will — reflect, then choose for yourself.
